title: SMBv1 Enabled with Null Session Access and SMB Signing Disabled
description: Samba 3.0.20-Debian has SMBv1 enabled, SMB signing not required, and allows anonymous/null session access. Null sessions expose 3 file shares (tmp, opt, print$) and 35 user accounts. SMBv1 is vulnerable to multiple critical exploits and lack of signing enables NTLM relay attacks.
evidence:
Exploitation:
$ smbclient -L //192.168.0.140 -N

Anonymous login successful

Sharename       Type      Comment
---------       ----      -------
print$          Disk      Printer Drivers
tmp             Disk      oh noes!
opt             Disk
IPC$            IPC       IPC Service (metasploitable server (Samba 3.0.20-Debian))
ADMIN$          IPC       IPC Service (metasploitable server (Samba 3.0.20-Debian))

Workgroup: WORKGROUP, Master: SMBSHARE

Null Session User Enumeration (35 accounts): root, msfadmin, user, service, postgres, mysql, www-data, distccd, ftp, etc.

Configuration: SMBv1 enabled (CRITICAL), SMB signing not required (HIGH)
remediation: Disable SMBv1. Enable and require SMB signing. Disable null session access. Restrict share access with proper ACLs. Upgrade Samba to a current supported version.

suggestions: Include session output or file access proof
